title: AgriInvest Program
body_text:
AgriInvest Program
1	Introduction to the AgriInvest Program
1.1	Overview of the AgriInvest Program
The AgriInvest Program is a government initiative that helps protect qualified agricultural producers from small income declines and that supports investments to help mitigate risks or improve market income. Agriculture and Agri-Foods Canada (AAFC) Administration is responsible for approving participation in the program and for handling AgriInvest related queries.
A Participant must open an AgriInvest Account to participate in the AgriInvest Program. The Participant can only open one AgriInvest Account for their farming operation. However, if an individual incorporates a farming operation and continues to operate an individual operation in addition to the corporation, they can have two AgriInvest Accounts, one for each operation.
1.2	Financial Institution Responsibilities
The majority of activities are handled directly between the Participant (the member) and AAFC Administration. The Administration makes all decisions regarding a Participantâs eligibility, deposit and withdrawal allowances, transfers of funds, and closures of accounts.
The Financial Institution is responsible for the following:
Enrolling as a participating AgriInvest Financial Institution with Central 1 (see Section 3.1, Participation Requirement for Financial Institutions)
Opening an AgriInvest Account for the Participant when the Participant presents an AgriInvest Account Initiation Form
Accepting and processing deposits from Participants
Note:	It is the Participantâs responsibility to meet the deposit deadline date shown on the Deposit Notice.
Processing deposits accurately by the deadline shown on the AgriInvest Deposit Notice
Important
:	Financial Institutions are responsible for any lost benefits resulting from their errors.
Paying out excess funds in a Participantâs AgriInvest Account if instructed with a Letter of Direction from AgriInvest Administration (see Section 6.2, Processing Deposits)
Processing transfer requests when a Participant presents an AgriInvest Transfer Form (see Section 5.3, Opening an AgriInvest Account to Receive a Transfer, and Section 6.4, Processing Transfers)
Maintaining a record of all AgriInvest Accounts, including opening and closing accounts, deposits and withdrawals, interest deposits, and error adjustments and submitting the information to Central 1 semi-monthly (see Chapter 6, AgriInvest Account Activity)
Note:	The Financial Institution must report the Participantâs PIN on the Activity File with each Reporting Period.
Providing Central 1 with a Financial Institution AgriInvest contact
The Financial Institution isnât responsible for the following:
Determining the Participantâs eligibility in the AgriInvest Program
Determining the eligibility of the Participantâs deposit
Answering member queries on the AgriInvest Program
An AgriInvest Account has two components. Fund 1 holds deposits made by the Participant. Fund 2 holds matching contributions from the government and all interest paid on the account. Funds can be withdrawn any time for risk mitigation or other investments. Withdrawals are taken from Fund 2 first and are taxable upon withdrawal. Withdrawals from Fund 1 arenât taxable.
Note:	If an account contains excess deposits, a withdrawal request must first be applied to the excess deposit amount, and then to Fund 2.
Deposits are made annually and must be made by the deposit deadline shown on the AgriInvest Deposit Notice. Only one deposit can be made per AgriInvest Deposit Notice. Participants can deposit funds up to 100% of their Allowable Net Sales. The Participant receives a matching contribution from the government for the first 1%, up to $10,000 per year.
1.3	AgriInvest Service Charges to the Financial Institution
A one-time administration fee for setting up a Financial Institution with AgriInvest reporting service includes the following:
Activity File Format Documentation
Excel tool for data capture and Activity File generation
Secure file transfer set-up
Training session on file format, Excel tool and processes
File upload testing
Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario can refer to the Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule, Form 2725, for more details and information on additional service charges.
